title: Write the Price You Want Next to the Price You Charge
subtitle: A short written exercise for naming the gap between your current rate and the one you actually want
excerpt: A two-minute writing activity: you write your current price and your wanted price side by side, then write one sentence about what sits in the gap. The gap, once named on paper, is easier to work with than the vague sense that something is off.

practice.whenToUse: Use when you know there is a gap between what you charge and what you want to charge, but haven't put the two numbers next to each other yet.

content.text: A two-minute writing exercise for the pricing gap. You write two numbers side by side: your current price and your wanted price. Then write one sentence about what lives between them. Named on paper, the gap is easier to work with than a vague sense that something is off.
New to activities as practice? Read

Pick one thing you sell or offer — a service, a product, an hourly rate. Write the current price. Below it, write the number you would charge if the wobble weren't there. Below that, write one sentence about what sits between them.
That sentence is the starting point. Use the journal prompts or tapping in this library to work with whatever it names.
